subject: everyday-science
description: Planets MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: How many planets are there in our solar system?
mcqs.explanation: There are 8 planets: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars (inner rocky/terrestrial) + Jupiter, Saturn (gas giants) + Uranus, Neptune (ice giants). Pluto was downgraded to dwarf planet in 2006.

mcqs.q: In which year did the International Astronomical Union (IAU) downgrade Pluto to a 'dwarf planet'?
mcqs.explanation: In 2006 the International Astronomical Union (IAU) downgraded the status of Pluto to that of 'dwarf planet'.

mcqs.q: Which 4 planets are inner rocky / terrestrial planets?
mcqs.explanation: The inner rocky / terrestrial planets are Mercury, Venus, Earth and Mars.

mcqs.q: Which planets are classified as gas giants?
mcqs.explanation: Jupiter and Saturn are gas giants. Uranus and Neptune are classified as ice giants.

mcqs.q: Which planets are classified as ice giants?
mcqs.explanation: Uranus and Neptune are ice giants. Jupiter and Saturn are gas giants.

mcqs.q: How many planets can be seen from Earth without a telescope?
mcqs.explanation: We can see 5 planets without a telescope. Venus can be easily seen with the naked eye.

mcqs.q: Which is the smallest planet in our solar system?
mcqs.explanation: Mercury is the smallest planet in our solar system — only slightly larger than Earth's Moon.

mcqs.q: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
mcqs.explanation: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun — about 36 million miles (58 million km) or 0.39 AU away.

mcqs.q: How long does one year on Mercury take in Earth days?
mcqs.explanation: Mercury makes a complete orbit around the Sun (a year) in just 88 Earth days.

mcqs.q: How long does one day on Mercury take (rotation with respect to stars)?
mcqs.explanation: One day on Mercury (the time it takes for Mercury to rotate or spin once with respect to the stars) takes 59 Earth days.

mcqs.q: How many moons does Mercury have?
mcqs.explanation: Mercury has no moons and no rings.

mcqs.q: Which spacecraft was the first to orbit the innermost planet (Mercury)?
mcqs.explanation: NASA's MESSENGER was the first spacecraft to orbit Mercury (Mariner 10 was the first to fly by it).

mcqs.q: Despite its proximity to the Sun, is Mercury the hottest planet?
options: No — Venus is hotter due to its thick CO₂ atmosphere
mcqs.explanation: Despite its proximity to the Sun, Mercury is NOT the hottest planet — Venus is, due to its thick CO₂ atmosphere causing a runaway greenhouse effect.

mcqs.q: Which is the hottest planet in the solar system?
mcqs.explanation: Venus is the hottest planet — surface temperature about 475°C (900°F).

mcqs.q: Which planet is known as Earth's twin?
mcqs.explanation: Venus is known as Earth's twin — similar in size and mass to Earth.

mcqs.q: Which planet is known as the morning star?
mcqs.explanation: Venus is known as the morning star — visible just before sunrise (and as evening star after sunset).

mcqs.q: Which is the brightest planet visible from Earth?
mcqs.explanation: Venus is the brightest planet — easily seen with the naked eye.

mcqs.q: How long does one day on Venus last in Earth days?
mcqs.explanation: One day on Venus lasts 243 Earth days — Venus spins backwards (east to west), with the Sun rising in the west and setting in the east.

mcqs.q: Venus's atmosphere is mostly composed of:
options: Carbon dioxide (greenhouse gas) with sulphuric acid clouds
mcqs.explanation: Venus has a thick atmosphere full of the greenhouse gas carbon dioxide and clouds made of sulphuric acid.

mcqs.q: Venus is one of just two planets that rotate from east to west. The other is:
mcqs.explanation: Venus and Uranus are the two planets that rotate from east to west (retrograde rotation).

mcqs.q: Earth is the ___ planet from the Sun.
mcqs.explanation: Earth is the third planet from the Sun, at a distance of about 93 million miles (150 million km) = 1 AU.

mcqs.q: How long does Earth take to complete one orbit around the Sun?
mcqs.explanation: Earth takes 365.25 days to complete one trip (orbit) around the Sun.

mcqs.q: In which direction does the Earth rotate around its axis?
mcqs.explanation: The Earth rotates around its axis from West to East — this causes the Sun to appear to rise in the east.

mcqs.q: Earth is composed of how many main layers?
mcqs.explanation: Earth has three layers: crust, mantle and core. Some classifications further divide the core into inner and outer core (4 layers total).

mcqs.q: What is the composition of Earth's atmosphere?
mcqs.explanation: Earth's atmosphere consists of 78% nitrogen, 21% oxygen and 1% other gases (argon, CO₂, neon).

mcqs.q: How many natural satellites (moons) does Earth have?
mcqs.explanation: Earth has one large natural satellite known as the Moon.

mcqs.q: Earth is also called the:
mcqs.explanation: Earth is known as the Blue Planet, Green Planet and Watery Planet — and is the only planet with an atmosphere supporting known life.

mcqs.q: What causes the seasons on Earth?
options: The Earth's movement (tilted axis) around the Sun
mcqs.explanation: The Earth's movement around the Sun (combined with its axial tilt) causes the seasons.

mcqs.q: What is 'aphelion'?
options: When Earth is closest to the Sun
Earth's position when at its greatest distance from the Sun (early July, Northern Hemisphere summer)
mcqs.explanation: Aphelion is the position of Earth in its orbit when it is at its greatest distance from the Sun — it occurs in early July, during Northern Hemisphere summer.

mcqs.q: Which is the most abundant element in the Earth's crust?
mcqs.explanation: Oxygen is the most abundant element in the Earth's crust (about 46.6% by mass).

mcqs.q: Mars is the ___ planet from the Sun.
mcqs.explanation: Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun — at an average distance of 142 million miles (228 million km), or 1.52 AU.

mcqs.q: Why is Mars called the Red Planet?
options: Iron minerals in Martian soil oxidise (rust), causing red colour
mcqs.explanation: Mars is known as the Red Planet because iron minerals in the Martian soil oxidise (rust), causing the soil and atmosphere to look red.

mcqs.q: How many moons does Mars have?
mcqs.explanation: Mars has two moons named Phobos and Deimos.

mcqs.q: Which is the second smallest planet in the solar system?
mcqs.explanation: Mars is the second smallest planet in the solar system (Mercury is the smallest).

mcqs.q: How long does one year on Mars take in Earth days?
mcqs.explanation: Mars takes 687 Earth days to complete a complete orbit around the Sun (a Martian year).

mcqs.q: Who was the first person to observe Mars with a telescope?
mcqs.explanation: Galileo Galilei was the first person to observe Mars with a telescope, in 1609.

mcqs.q: Which is the largest planet in the solar system?
mcqs.explanation: Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system. Eleven Earths could fit across Jupiter's equator.

mcqs.q: Approximately how many Earths could fit across Jupiter's equator?
mcqs.explanation: Eleven Earths could fit across Jupiter's equator.

mcqs.q: Jupiter is classified as a:
mcqs.explanation: Jupiter is known as a gas giant — its atmosphere is made up mostly of hydrogen (H₂) and helium (He).

mcqs.q: How long does one day on Jupiter (Jovian day) last?
mcqs.explanation: Jupiter rotates once about every 10 hours (a Jovian day) — the fastest spin in the solar system.

mcqs.q: How long does Jupiter take to complete one orbit of the Sun?
mcqs.explanation: Jupiter takes about 12 Earth years to complete one orbit of the Sun (a Jovian year).

mcqs.q: Jupiter's Great Red Spot is:
options: A gigantic storm about twice the size of Earth, raging for over a century
mcqs.explanation: Jupiter's Great Red Spot is a gigantic storm about twice the size of Earth that has raged for over a century.

mcqs.q: Saturn is the ___ planet from the Sun.
mcqs.explanation: Saturn is the sixth planet from the Sun — about 9.5 AU away (886 million miles / 1.4 billion km).

mcqs.q: Which planet has the most spectacular ring system (7 rings)?
mcqs.explanation: Saturn has the most spectacular ring system, with seven rings (in 7 groups) and several gaps between them.

mcqs.q: Saturn is also known as the ___ planet.
mcqs.explanation: Saturn is the flattest planet — its rapid rotation causes it to bulge at the equator.

mcqs.q: How long does Saturn take to orbit the Sun?
mcqs.explanation: Saturn makes a complete orbit around the Sun (a year in Saturnian time) in about 29.4 Earth years (10,756 Earth days).

mcqs.q: Which is the largest moon of Saturn?
mcqs.explanation: Titan is the largest moon of Saturn — and the second-largest moon in the solar system.

mcqs.q: Saturn is composed mostly of:
mcqs.explanation: Saturn is a massive ball made mostly of hydrogen and helium — it is a gas-giant planet.

mcqs.q: Uranus is the ___ planet from the Sun.
mcqs.explanation: Uranus is the seventh planet from the Sun — about 1.8 billion miles (2.9 billion km) away, or 19.8 AU.

mcqs.q: Which was the first planet found using a telescope?
mcqs.explanation: Uranus was the first planet found using a telescope — discovered in 1781 by William Herschel.

mcqs.q: Who discovered Uranus?
mcqs.explanation: Uranus was discovered in 1781 by William Herschel using a telescope.

mcqs.q: Uranus is an example of which type of planet?
mcqs.explanation: Uranus is an Ice Giant planet — surrounded by 13 faint rings.

mcqs.q: How many known moons does Uranus have?
mcqs.explanation: Uranus has 27 known moons — most of which are named after literary characters. Titania is the largest.

mcqs.q: Neptune is the ___ planet from the Sun.
mcqs.explanation: Neptune is the eighth (and outermost) planet from the Sun — about 2.8 billion miles (4.5 billion km) away, or 30 AU.

mcqs.q: Which is the coldest planet in the solar system?
mcqs.explanation: Neptune is the coldest planet — average temperature about -200°C (-392°F).

mcqs.q: Neptune was discovered by:
options: Urbain Le Verrier and Johann Galle
mcqs.explanation: Neptune was discovered by Urbain Le Verrier and Johann Galle in 1846 — predicted mathematically before being observed.

mcqs.q: Which planet lies at the OUTERMOST orbit of the solar system?
mcqs.explanation: Neptune is the planet at the outermost orbit of the solar system. (Pluto is no longer classified as a planet — it is a dwarf planet.)

mcqs.q: Which planet of the solar system has water vapours?
mcqs.explanation: Per the source material, Neptune is the only planet of the solar system noted as having water vapours in this context.
